member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ComponentsWebViewServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: Extensions for adding component webview services to the .

member.summary: Adds component webview services to the collection.
member.param: The to add the component webview services to.

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider
member.summary: Provides a mapping between file extensions and MIME types.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new provider with a set of default mappings.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.String})
member.summary: Creates a lookup engine using the provided mapping. It is recommended that the IDictionary instance use StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase.

member.name: P:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider.Mappings
member.summary: The cross reference table of file extensions and content-types.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.FileExtensionContentTypeProvider.TryGetContentType(System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Given a file path, determine the MIME type
member.returns: True if MIME type could be determined

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.IContentTypeProvider
member.summary: Used to look up MIME types given a file path

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.IContentTypeProvider.TryGetContentType(System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Given a file path, determine the MIME type
member.returns: True if MIME type could be determined

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.PageContext
member.summary:
Represents the services that are scoped to a single page load. Grouping them like this means we don't have to check that each of them are available individually.
This has roughly the same role as a circuit in Blazor Server. One key difference is that, for web views, the IPC channel is outside the page context, whereas in Blazor Server, the IPC channel is within the circuit.

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager
member.summary: Manages activities within a web view that hosts Razor components. Platform authors should subclass this to wire up the abstract and protected methods to the APIs of the platform's web view.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.#ctor(System.IServiceProvider,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Dispatcher,System.Uri,Microsoft.Extensions.FileProviders.IFileProvider,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.JSComponentConfigurationStore,System.String)
member.summary: Constructs an instance of .
member.param: A instance that can marshal calls to the required thread or sync context.
The base URI for the application. Since this is a webview, the base URI is typically on a private origin such as http://0.0.0.0/ or app://example/
Provides static content to the webview.
Describes configuration for adding, removing, and updating root components from JavaScript code.
Path to the host page within the .

member.name: P:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.Dispatcher
member.summary: Gets the used by this instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.Navigate(System.String)
member.summary: Instructs the web view to navigate to the specified location, bypassing any client-side routing.
member.param: The URL, which may be absolute or relative to the application root.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.NavigateCore(System.Uri)
member.summary: Instructs the web view to navigate to the specified location, bypassing any client-side routing.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.SendMessage(System.String)
member.summary: Sends a message to JavaScript code running in the attached web view. This must be forwarded to the Blazor JavaScript code.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.AddRootComponentAsync(System.Type,System.String,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.ParameterView)
member.summary: Adds a root component to the attached page.
member.param: The type of the root component. This must implement .
The CSS selector describing where in the page the component should be placed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.RemoveRootComponentAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a previously-attached root component from the current page.
member.param: The CSS selector describing where in the page the component was placed. This must exactly match the selector provided on an earlier call to .

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.MessageReceived(System.Uri,System.String)
member.summary: Notifies the about a message from JavaScript running within the web view.
member.param: The source URI for the message.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.TryDispatchAsync(System.Action{System.IServiceProvider})
member.summary: Calls the specified asynchronously and passes in the scoped services available to Razor components. This method will not throw any exceptions if it is unable to call the specified , but if it does call it, then exceptions may still be thrown by the itself.
member.returns: Returns a representing true if the was called, or false if it was not called because Blazor is not currently running.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.TryGetResponseContent(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32@,System.String@,System.IO.Stream@,System.Collections.Generic.IDictionary{System.String,System.String}@)
member.summary: Tries to provide the response content for a given network request.
member.param: Whether or not to fallback to the host page.
The status code of the response.
member.returns: true if the response can be provided; false otherwise.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.WebViewManager.DisposeAsyncCore
member.summary: Disposes the current instance.

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1
member.summary:
Implements a list that uses an array of objects to store the elements.
This differs from a in that it not only grows as required but also shrinks if cleared with significant excess capacity. This makes it useful for component rendering, because components can be long-lived and re-render frequently, with the rendered size varying dramatically depending on the user's navigation in the app.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Buffers.ArrayPool{`0})
member.summary: Constructs a new instance of .

member.name: P:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of items.

member.name: P:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.Buffer
member.summary: Gets the underlying buffer.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.Append(`0@)
member.summary: Appends a new item, automatically resizing the underlying array if necessary.
member.returns: The index of the appended item.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.Overwrite(System.Int32,`0@)
member.summary: Sets the supplied value at the specified index. The index must be within range for the array.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.RemoveLast
member.summary: Removes the last item.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.InsertExpensive(System.Int32,`0)
member.summary: Inserts the item at the specified index, moving the contents of the subsequent entries along by one.
member.param: The index at which the value is to be inserted.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilder`1.Clear
member.summary: Marks the array as empty, also shrinking the underlying storage if it was not being used to near its full capacity.

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.RenderBatchWriter
member.summary:
Provides a custom binary serializer for instances. This is designed with both server-side and client-side perf in mind:
* Array-like regions always have a fixed size per entry (even if some entry types don't require as much space as others) so the recipient can index directly. * The indices describing where field data starts, where each string value starts, etc., are written *after* that data, so when writing the data we don't have to compute the locations up front or seek back to an earlier point in the stream. The recipient can only process the data after reading it all into a buffer, so it's no disadvantage for the location info to be at the end. * We only serialize the data that the JS side will need. For example, we don't emit frame sequence numbers, or any representation of nonstring attribute values, or component instances, etc.
We don't have or need a .NET reader for this format. We only read it from JS code.

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.ArrayBuilderMemoryStream
member.summary: Writeable memory stream backed by a an .

member.name: P:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.Resources.Exception_PathMustStartWithSlash
member.summary: The path in '{0}' must start with '/'.

member.name: M:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebView.Resources.FormatException_PathMustStartWithSlash(System.Object)
member.summary: The path in '{0}' must start with '/'.

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.PullFromJSDataStream
member.Summary: A stream that pulls each chunk on demand using JavaScript interop. This implementation is used for WebAssembly and WebView applications.

member.name: T:Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.TransmitDataStreamToJS
member.Summary: A stream that pulls each chunk on demand using JavaScript interop. This implementation is used for WebAssembly and WebView applications.
